claim: Prescribed exercise training improves quality of life.
evidence_sentences: At 3 months, usual care plus exercise training led to greater improvement in the KCCQ overall summary score (mean, 5.21; 95% confidence interval, 4.42 to 6.00) compared with usual care alone (3.28; 95% confidence interval, 2.48 to 4.09).

After 3 months, there were no further significant changes in KCCQ score for either group (P = .85 for the difference between slopes), resulting in a sustained, greater improvement overall for the exercise group (P < .001).
CONCLUSIONS Exercise training conferred modest but statistically significant improvements in self-reported health status compared with usual care without training.
